LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS EQUINOX FITNESS SC SAN FRANCISCO, INC. AND EQUINOX HOLDINGS, INC.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED AS TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR RETALIATION IN VIOLATION OF CIVIL CODE 51-52. A SPLIT IN AUTHORITY EXISTS, BUT I FIND VAUGHN V. HUGO NEU PROLER INT'L (1990) 223 CAL.APP.3D 1612 AND THE DISSENT IN GAYER V. POLK GULCH, INC. (1991) 231 CAL.APP.3D 515 TO BE BETTER REASONED THAN THE MAJORITY OPINION IN THE LATTER CASE. THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR RETALIATION IN VIOLATION OF CIVIL CODE 51.7; PLAINTIFF CITES NO AUTHORITY HOLDING THAT THE PLEADED CAUSE OF ACTION EXISTS. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER; CLERK: G. GONZALES; REPORTER: REYES F. HUNTER CSR#6576, EMAIL: RHUNTER@SFTC.ORG.

calendar.matters:
Plaintiff Colin Heilbuts Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Defendant Equinox Holdings, Inc.S Memorandum Of Costs Or, In The Alternative, Tax Costs; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
Off Calendar May-24-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF COLIN HEILBUT Plaintiff Colin Heilbuts' Motion To Strike Defendant Equinox Holdings, Inc.S Memorandum Of Costs Or, In The Alternative, Tax Costs; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof

Plaintiff Colin Heilbut's motion to strike defendant Equinox Holdings, Inc.'s memorandum of costs is DENIED. Plaintiff's alternative motion to tax costs is GRANTED. The parties agree that the costs sought for witness and electronic-document-hosting fees are excessive. Those costs are thus taxed $4,430, reducing the award to $23,228. As explained below, defendant is due those remaining costs.

"Except as otherwise expressly provided by statute, a prevailing party is entitled as a matter of right to recover costs in any action or proceeding." (CCP 1032(b).) The Unruh Act provides that only prevailing plaintiffs may obtain attorney fees, saying nothing about costs. (Civ. Code 52(a).) Roman v. BRE Properties, Inc. (2015) 237 Cal.App.4th 1040, 1059 holds that defendants may recover Unruh Act costs, because the act lacks cost-shifting language. The cases plaintiff cites are not to the contrary. Turner v. Ass'n. of American Medical Colleges (2011) 193 Cal.App.4th 1047, 1059-60 and D.C. v. Harvard-Westlake School (2009) 176 Cal.App.4th 836, 864-65 only address attorney fees, not costs. Williams v. Chino Valley Independent Fire District (2015) 61 Cal.4th 97, 115 is a Fair Employment and Housing Act case, and the FEHA expressly denies fees and costs to defendants unless the suit is found frivolous. (Gov't. Code 12965(b).)

Plaintiff's financial condition is not a proper consideration in deciding whether a defendant's Unruh Act costs are reasonable. (LAOSD Asbestos Cases (2018) 25 Cal.App.5th 1116, 1122-26.) The cases plaintiff cites are under FEHA or regard CCP 998.

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR# 8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. (302/RBU)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Defendant Equinox Holdings, Inc. For Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative, For Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Feb-26-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Noah Baron, Esq. and Larry Organ, Esq. 415-453-4740 of California Civil Rights Law Group for Plaintiff.
Dylan Carp. Esq. 415-394-9400 of Jackson Lewis P.C. for Defendant Equinox.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court shares it's views. Plaintiff argues vicarious liability, ratification, Defendant argues. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT EQUINOX FITNESS SC SAN FRANCISCO, INC., EQUINOX HOLDINGS, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, For Summary Adjudication.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Defendant Equinox Holdings, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is granted.

Causes of action 1, 3, 5, and 9 all fail because Equinox is not vicariously liable for the alleged sexual assault as a matter of law. Equinox has a policy against sexual assault and such conduct is not part of normal yoga instruction. (Plaintiff's Response to UMF 2.) The fact that it is foreseeable that yoga instruction can involve touching does not create liability. (Lisa M. v. Henry Mayo Newhall Memorial Hospital (1995) 12 Cal.4th 291, 302 ["In our view, that a job involves physical contact is, by itself, an insufficient basis on which to impose vicarious liability for a sexual assault."].) Plaintiff fails to create a triable issue of material fact that Equinox ratified the sexual assault. Mr. Nguyen was terminated 6 days after Plaintiff lodged his complaint. (Cf. Delfino v. Agilent Technologies, Inc. (2006) 145 Cal.App.4th 790, 811 [affirming summary judgment for employer which placed employee suspected of making online threats on administrative leave immediately after interview and terminated him eight days later].) Moreover, it is Plaintiff's position that Equinox "terminated Defendant Nguyen's employment for gross misconduct because it found [Plaintiff's] claim of sexual assault plausible and feared liability." (Plaintiff's Response to UMF 42.) This assertion undermines Plaintiff's ratification theory.

Cause of action 2 fails because under the weight of recent authority, the Unruh Civil Rights Act "does not encompass discrimination based on retaliation." (Gayer v. Polk Gulch, Inc. (1991) 231 Cal.App.3d 515, 519-525 [claim that defendant bar excluded plaintiff in retaliation for his having previously filed a discrimination suit against its owner failed to state a cause of action under the Unruh Act]; see also Scripps Clinic v. Superior Court (2003) 108 Cal.App.4th 917, 931-35 [finding that defendant medical group's policy of terminating medical care for patients who file lawsuits against its physicians did not constitute retaliatory discrimination that the Unruh Act was meant to preclude, as the policy "does not rely upon a patient's personal characteristics, but upon that patient's conduct"]; Moralez v. Whole Foods Market California, Inc. (N.D. Cal. Dec. 9, 2015) 2015 WL 8292079 at *4 [even assuming that plaintiff was subjected to disparate treatment, through retaliation, because she had litigated a prior civil rights case against defendant in the past, this does not give rise to an offense under the Unruh Act].)

Cause of action 6 fails because the undisputed evidence establishes that Mr. Nguyen had no prior history of committing a sexual assault and Equinox had no notice of such a history. (Plaintiff's Response to UMF 4.) Plaintiff does not address this cause of action in his opposition papers.(as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

OBJECTION HEARING CALENDAR
Off Calendar Mar-14-2018 Off Calendar

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-05-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Dylan B. Carp, Esq. of Jackson Lewis, T:415.394.9400, for defendant/moving party;
Navruz Avloni, Esq. of California Civil Rights Law Group, T:415.453.4740, for plaintiff/opposing party.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS EQUINOX FITNESS SC SAN FRANCISCO, INC. and EQUINOX HOLDINGS, INC.'s DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is OVERRULED as to the second cause of action for retaliation in violation of Civil Code 51-52. A split in authority exists, but I find Vaughn v. Hugo Neu Proler Int'l (1990) 223 Cal.App.3d 1612 and the dissent in Gayer v. Polk Gulch, Inc. (1991) 231 Cal.App.3d 515 to be better reasoned than the majority opinion in the latter case. The demurrer is SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND as to the fourth cause of action for retaliation in violation of Civil Code 51.7; plaintiff cites no authority holding that the pleaded cause of action exists.

Prevailing party to prepare and submit a proposed order. Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Reyes F. Hunter CSR#6576, email: rhunter@sftc.org.
